response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior software engineer with decades of full-stack, web, and some low-latency systems experience, but the resume shows no evidence of the core requirements for this role: SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator or GPU architecture work, deep-learning workload analysis, or hardware-software co-design. The closest relevant experience (low-latency DLang services, C/C++ tooling at National Instruments, embedded mentions) is adjacent at best and dated. This is a domain mismatch for a Principal Performance Architect position despite the candidate's overall seniority.
strengths: 25+ years of software engineering experience, easily exceeding the Bachelor's + 8 years requirement
Proven low-latency, high-throughput systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching at millions of requests/sec)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and technical community leadership
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon architecture, or hardware performance modeling experience
No deep-learning training/inference optimization, GPU, SIMD/MIMD, or AI accelerator background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, not hardware-software co-design or system architecture for accelerators
